pattern: An interval dynamic program over prefixes and remaining groups: the best score for a prefix using g groups is the average of a trailing block plus the best score for the rest using g-1 groups.
hints: Prefix sums let you take the average of any contiguous block in constant time.
Define the best achievable score for the first i elements split into at most g groups, and let the last group be some suffix of that prefix.
For each cut point, combine the average of the final block with the optimum of the earlier portion using one fewer group, taking the maximum over all cut points.

edge_inputs.description: isolating the two large ends maximizes the score
four groups over a sorted run
k equals one forces a single average over everything
k equals length puts each element in its own group
extra allowed groups do not help when values are equal

stress.description: random arrays with a valid k between one and the length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: k constrained to the valid range so the partition is always feasible
